author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
title: The Eighth Breviate.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: “Again, this question must be asked,” he says, “how God wishes man to be,—with sin, or without sin?

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Beyond doubt, He does not wish him to be with sin.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: We must reflect how great would be the impious blasphemy for it to be said that man has it in his power to be with sin, which God does not wish; and for it to be denied that he has it in his power to be without sin, which God wishes: just as if God had created any man for such a result as this,—that he should be able to be what He would not have him, and unable to be what He would have him; and that he should lead an existence contrary to His will, rather than one which should be in accordance therewith.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: This has been in fact already answered; but I see that it is necessary for me to make here an additional remark, that we are saved by hope.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: “But hope that is seen is not hope; for what a man seeth, why doth he yet hope for?

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: But if we hope for that we see not, then do we with patience wait for it.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Full righteousness, therefore, will only then be reached, when fulness of health is attained; and this fulness of health shall be when there is fulness of love, for “love is the fulfilling of the law;” and then shall come fulness of love, when “we shall see Him even as He is.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Nor will any addition to love be possible more, when faith shall have reached the fruition of sight.
